steps.text: Consult either the air permitting authority for the entity or your EPA Regional representative as listed in 40 CFR 63.13.
File a petition for review in the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit.

preconditions.text: If you have any questions regarding the applicability of this action to a particular entity
Only an objection to this final rule that was raised with reasonable specificity during the period for public comment can be raised during judicial review

constraints.text: The petition for review must be filed by May 30, 2023.

steps.text: Introduce the reference gas into the sampling system.

constraints.text: The reference gas must be introduced prior to any sample conditioning or filtration equipment.
The reference gas must pass through as much of the probe as is practical.

steps.text: Submit a rule report to each house of the Congress and to the Comptroller General of the United States.

Measure the first hour of particulate matter emissions for each test run by sampling with a third, identical and independent sampling train operated concurrently for the first hour of PM paired train compliance testing.
Report the test results from this third train separately as the first hour emissions.

preconditions.text: For all tests conducted using ASTM E2515–11 (with the exceptions described in paragraphs (c)(1) and (2) of this section, and pursuant to this section)

constraints.text: Immediately after component changes, leak checks are optional but highly recommended. If such leak checks are done, the procedure in paragraph (c)(1) of this section should be used.

steps.text: Amend § 60.5474 by revising paragraphs (b)(2), (3), and (6).
Amend § 60.5476 by removing paragraphs (c)(5) and (6).
Amend § 60.5476 by revising paragraph (f).
Determine the location and layout of the traverse points (minimum 40 for circular ducts, 42 for rectangular ducts) following the procedure outlined in section 11.3 and table 1-1 or 1-2.
Use a directional flow-sensing probe to measure pitch and yaw angles of the gas flow at the traverse points.
Calculate the resultant angle and compare with acceptable criteria for mean and standard deviation.
If the alternative measurement location is determined to be acceptable, use the same traverse points for future sampling and velocity measurements.

preconditions.text: Measurement locations are less than 2 equivalent or duct diameters downstream or less than one-half duct diameter upstream from a flow disturbance.
Ducts are larger than 24 inches in diameter where blockage and wall effects are minimal.

constraints.text: Both the pitch and yaw angles must be measured from a line passing through the traverse point and parallel to the stack axis.
The pitch angle is the angle of the gas flow component in the plane that includes the traverse line and is parallel to the stack axis.

The yaw angle is the angle of the gas flow component in the plane perpendicular to the traverse line at the traverse point and is measured from the line passing through the traverse point and parallel to the stack axis.

steps.text: Purchase an opacity monitor that meets the specifications of ASTM D6216–20, including a suitable data recorder or automated data acquisition handling system.

preconditions.text: If the initial certification of the opacity monitor occurred before May 30, 2023, using D6216–98, D6216–03, D6216–07, or D6216–12, it is not necessary to recertify using D6216–20.

constraints.text: The data recorder must have an input signal range compatible with the analyzer output.

steps.text: Conduct the CD test at the two points specified in section 6.1.2 of this method.
Introduce to the CEMS the reference gases, gas cells, or optical filters.

Introduce the reference gas prior to any sample conditioning or filtration equipment and ensure that it passes through all filters, scrubbers, conditioners, and other monitor components used during normal sampling.
Record the CEMS response and subtract this value from the reference value.
Calculate the RA, expressed as a percentage, of a set of data.

constraints.text: The reference gas should pass through as much of the sampling probe as practical.

preconditions.text: Establish appropriate safety and health practices and determine the applicable regulatory limitations prior to performing this performance specification.
Consult the CEMS user's manual for specific precautions to be taken with regard to the analytical procedures.

constraints.text: This performance specification may involve hazardous materials, operations, and equipment.

steps.text: Install the CEMS.
Prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in section 8.1 of this method.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Use a dual probe system to determine the average effluent concentration while measurements at each traverse point are being made to determine whether effluent stratification exists.

preconditions.text: If installing CO and O2 monitors at different locations, ensure the effluent gases at both sample locations are not stratified and there is no in-leakage of air between sampling locations.

constraints.text: Both the CO and O2 monitors should be installed at the same general location if possible.
For Point CEMS, the measurement point should be within or centrally located over the centroidal area of the stack or duct cross section.
For Path CEMS, the effective measurement path must meet the specified cross-sectional area coverage requirements.

When pollutant concentration changes are due solely to diluent leakage and CO and O2 are simultaneously measured at the same location, one half diameter may be used in place of two equivalent diameters.

steps.text: Calculate the RD.
Certify your PEMS after initial installation and periodically thereafter.
Test the diluent component.
Conduct the RATA as described in section 8.2 of this method.
Conduct the applicable ongoing tests listed in this section.

preconditions.text: Paired samples are taken using RM 30B, RM 29, or ASTM D6784–16.
You intend to use a PEMS to show compliance with your emission limitation under this part or 40 CFR part 61 or 63.
PEMS contains a diluent (O2 or CO2) measuring component.
At least once every four calendar quarters after the initial certification test.
PEMS is installed under this part and 40 CFR parts 61 and 63 after May 30, 2023.

constraints.text: Express RD as a percentage or, for very low RM concentrations (≤1.0 μg/m3), as the absolute difference between Ca and Cb.

steps.text: Pass the reference gas through as much of the sampling probe as practical, ensuring that it passes through all filters, scrubbers, conditioners, and other monitor components used during normal sampling.
Adjust the CEMS calibration.
Calculate the accuracy for the RAA using equation 1-1 of this method.

preconditions.text: The daily zero (or low-level) CD or the daily high-level CD exceeds two times the limits of the applicable PS’s in appendix B to this part.

constraints.text: The RAA must be calculated in the units of the applicable emission standard.

steps.text: Perform the weekly system integrity check at least once every 7 operating days using a single mid- or high-level oxidized Hg reference gas to assess transport and measurement of oxidized mercury.
Conduct a Relative Accuracy Audit (RAA) following the RATA test procedures in section 8.5 of PS 12A in appendix B, using only three test runs.
Calculate the relative accuracy according to equation 1–1 of Procedure 1 of this appendix.
Redesignate paragraphs (d) through (t) as paragraphs (e) through (u).
Add new paragraph (d).
Revise newly redesignated paragraphs (i)(103) and (104).

preconditions.text: The RAA is conducted as an alternative to the QGA, and may be conducted in three of four calendar quarters, but in no more than three quarters in succession.

constraints.text: The absolute value of the difference between the Hg CEMS output response and the reference gas must be less than or equal to 10.0 percent of the reference gas value or 0.8 mg/scm.
